schools.name: Dyer Elementary School
schools.notes: PreK pilot site (29 students). Roof repair needed: $535K.

schools.name: James Otis Kaler Elementary School
schools.address: 165 South Kelsey Street, South Portland, ME 04106
schools.notes: PreK pilot site (29 students). Proposed for closure. Roof repair needed: $302K. Smallest K-4 enrollment.

schools.name: Dora L. Small Elementary School
schools.notes: No PreK program. Smallest school per budget documents (190 cited, NCES shows 199).

schools.name: Waldo T. Skillin Elementary School
schools.notes: Largest elementary. Most diverse student body. Most expensive capital needs (HVAC). Cannot be closed — remaining 4 schools lack capacity to absorb.

schools.name: South Portland Middle School
schools.notes: Staggered bell times (7:55 and 8:45). Community School model site. Bell time consolidation deferred to fall 2027 (requires $25K DOT traffic study).

geographic_notes.i295_impact: Interstate 295 bisects South Portland, separating west-side neighborhoods from east-side schools. West-side students are 100% bused. Some bus routes reach 45+ minutes.
geographic_notes.walkability_threshold: The redistricting tool uses 0.75 miles as the walkability threshold, accounting for actual road/sidewalk paths via OpenStreetMap data.
geographic_notes.walkable_percentage: Under current 5-school configuration, approximately 44% of students are walkable. This varies by closure scenario.

option_b.description: 4 buildings K-4, Kaler closed, redistrict by proximity

variant_c.description: 3 buildings PreK-2 + 1 building Grades 3-4, Kaler closed
